codeReview.summary: Clean implementation that fulfills all 5 assertions. Dependencies react-markdown (^10.1.0) and jszip (^3.10.1) added to package.json. BrainstormPage placeholder created with correct dark theme styling. Route added in both Electron and web blocks before the catch-all. IconBrainstorm animated SVG icon follows existing CustomIcons pattern with motion.svg, brain paths, and pulsing sparks. LandingPage updated with amber-themed brainstorm card, grid expanded from 4 to 5 columns, and click handler uses window.location.hash for navigation. All 4 baseline validators passed (ruff, pytest 53 tests, build, lint with 0 new errors). One minor non-blocking style note: the brainstorm card navigation uses `window.location.hash = '#/brainstorm'` instead of react-router-dom's `useNavigate` — this works fine with HashRouter but bypasses router transition lifecycle. Not a bug, just a slight inconsistency with how other SPA navigation might be done.

issues.description: Navigation uses `window.location.hash = '#/brainstorm'` instead of react-router-dom's `useNavigate()` hook. This works correctly with HashRouter but bypasses the router's transition system. The existing walkthrough card uses `onWalkthroughOpen()` (a callback prop) and section cards use `onNavigate()` (also a callback), so there's no consistent pattern to follow here anyway. Functional, just a minor style note.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used `window.location.hash` for programmatic navigation to the brainstorm route rather than react-router-dom's `useNavigate`. The mission AGENTS.md doesn't document a preferred navigation pattern for inter-page routing in the LandingPage. This could lead to inconsistency as more routes are added.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: frontend/src/pages/LandingPage.tsx:356 — `window.location.hash = '#/brainstorm'`. Compare to walkthrough which uses a callback prop `onWalkthroughOpen()` and section cards which use `onNavigate(card.key as SectionId)`. No documented convention for which approach to use.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill says to 'Write Tests FIRST (Red Phase)' in step 2, with an exception that 'Frontend: No test framework configured'. Worker correctly skipped frontend tests. Skill feedback suggests making this skip more explicit for frontend-only features — reasonable suggestion.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The fullstack-worker skill mentions writing tests FIRST (Red Phase) for backend, but for frontend-only features with no test framework this step is naturally skipped. The skill could explicitly note that frontend-only features skip the test-first phase.'

summary: Feature passes review. All 5 validation assertions are satisfied: react-markdown and jszip installed (CC-DB-01, CC-DB-02), BrainstormPage route exists at /#/brainstorm (FE-RN-01), LandingPage has Brainstorm Mode navigation card with amber theme and IconBrainstorm icon (FE-RN-02), and route is accessible in both Electron and web mode blocks (FE-RN-03). The implementation is clean, follows existing patterns, and all baseline validators pass. One minor non-blocking style note about navigation method.
